Concerns we're hearing from customers are often around ensuring that AI systems are acting as intended based on the audience in question. For example, you might want to ensure that your application is only discussing topics relevant to children. Or you might want to help ensure that your product isn't creating posts with potentially offensive content. AI adversarial testing is our answer to application developers looking to test their generative AI implementations to ensure they're safe for users. This system replaces manual red teamming efforts performed by organizations today with an automated system. This system generates a wide range of adversarial prompts using an unfiltered model. The tool then takes these prompts and feeds them to your LLM, capturing any responses it receives. The response are then evaluated based on the content policies and a report is drafted that can be downloaded and shared with your team. This falls into a wider group of technical mitigations which best-in-class teams are deploying in order to ensure risk mitigation at every stage of generative AI integration and deployment. This also fits within Google's wider set of tooling to help developers in this area. In particular, once users receive their AI adversarial testing report, they can share it with their team and go to model armor in order to take advantage of capabilities such as AI guardrails, prompt injection protection, jailbreak protection, and more. We can draw an analogy here between AI guardrails, and a network firewall. Adversarial testing is a way to look for potential issues, and model armor lets you protect your systems in production.
